metadata.title:
SPOKANE TELLS
NO HARD LUCK


HISTORY

City Maintained Progress
in Splendid Style


Last Year.

pages.raw_text:
Spokane banking records for the
year 1908, as registered in the
clearing house show a total clear
ing of 1907, 791, 4S2. This repre-
sents a gain of $6,372,460 for the
year. or an average gain of $780,
03S per month for the entire period.
This financial showing for the
city was made in what is regarded
the country over as one of the
tightest years from a monetary
standpoint that the country has ex-
perienced in the past decade and
more.


The same tone is apparent in all
lines of local industry. Manufac-
turing marks a 10 per cent general
increase and 20 per cent gain is
noted in the jobbing trade.


Building permits for the year
show an increase of $14S,672 in
value. From directory estimates
the population is figured today at
10000. an increase of $4,000 over
last year.


Postal revenues increased from
$302,3SS in 1907 to $34S,944 in 1908


Value of farm products in the
country tributary to Spokane is
estimated at $51,410,000, of which
wheat is worth $36,160.000. This is
. crop value gain of 20 per cent
over last year The value of the
lumber production in the same gen.
eral territory was $1S,00O,000, this
branch of industry showing a loss
on account Of the long prevailing
dullness of the eastern building
material market.


New stores and a marked expan-
sion in many already established
In Spokane prove a substantial
gain in the retail trade.


In every line conferred in Spo-
kane, there is nothing but the
spirit of better prospects than ever
for the year opening today. From
all indications the story the rec
ords for 1909 will tell one year
from today will be the grandest
one of progress the life of the city
has yet written.
